senses.gloss: The twenty-sixth letter of the Arabic alphabet, written in the Arabic script; preceded by ن (n) and followed by و (w).
The abjad numeral representing the number five (5)
him, his, it, its (bound object pronoun, referring to inanimate nouns of masculine gender)
Banū Ṭayyiʔ form of أَ (ʔa, interrogative particle)

etymology.text:
From the Nabataean letter 𐢇 (l, “he”), derived from the Phoenician letter 𐤄 (l, “he”), from the Egyptian hieroglyph 𓀠. See also Classical Syriac ܗ (l, “he”), Hebrew ה (l, “he”), Ancient Greek Ε (E), Latin E.

From Proto-Semitic *ha. Cognate to Hebrew הֲ־ (hă-).

entries.lexicon_en: Arabic-English Lexicon by Edward William Lane (d. 1876)
entries.lexicon_ar: المعجم العربي الإنجليزي لإدوارد وليام لين
entries.body_html:
ه alphabetical letter ه

[The twenty-sixth letter of the alphabet; called

هَآءٌ: it is one of the class termed حَلْقِيَّةٌ [or guttural], and is a radical letter, except when written with two dots, ة; for which, and for the pronominal values of ه, &c., see the Supplement.

b2: As a numeral it denotes five.]

entries.lexicon_en: Habib Anthony Salmone, An Advanced Learner's Arabic-English Dictionary (1889)
entries.lexicon_ar: حبيب سالمون، قاموس عربي انجليزي متقدم للمتعلمين
entries.body_html:
ه
ه
a. Hā
The twenty-sixth letter of the alphabet. Its numerical value is Five, (5).
هْ
a. Sometimes suffixed to exclamative expressions.
يَا أَسَفَاهْ O sorrow!

هُِ (a. pers. or pass. pron. suffixed ), Him.
b. His.

هَا
a. Her.
b. Hers.

هِيَ
a. She.

هَآء
a. There! Take!
b. Behold! Lo! Here!

هَاكَِ
a. Take!

هَاءَنَذَا
a. Behold me! Here I am!

هَاهُنَا
a. Here! This way!

هَا اللّهُ
a. By God! In God's Name!

هَارُوْن
a. Haroun; Aaron.

entries.lexicon_en: Arabic-English Lexicon by Edward William Lane (d. 1876)
entries.lexicon_ar: المعجم العربي الإنجليزي لإدوارد وليام لين
entries.body_html:
(Supplement) alphabetical letter ه

هُ: the vowel of this pron. is sometimes, in a case of pause, transferred to the preceding letter: and this is always done in modern Arabic.

b2: هْ subjoined in هُنَّهْ, and the like: see أَبْيَضُ.

b3: The هَا, termed تَنْبِيهٌ, is said by some to be used as an inceptive, without any meaning but inception. (See ذا.) See كَلِمَةُ تَنْبِيهٍ in art. نبه: and see an ex. voce تَا. When followed by a pronoun, as هو, &c., it is best rendered Lo.

b4: ه of pausation: see remarks on a verse cited voce حِينٌ.

b5: بِهَا, in حَطَأَ بِهَا, and many phrases syn. therewith, is app. for بِاسْتِهِ. See حَطَأَ.

هَآءَ and هَآءِ: see art. هوأ.
